description: Bug: the store micro-frontend and product detail page fail to load. Root cause confirmed by code analysis: `micro-frontends/host/src/microFe/MicroFrontendApp.tsx:93` calls `@module-federation/runtime` `init()` with `name: '@ecommerce-host'` on every render, but `@nx/module-federation` already initialised the MF runtime at build-time (name: 'host' from module-federation.config.ts). Calling `init()` again with a different name throws #RUNTIME-010, which ErrorBoundary catches and shows as 'Failed to Load Micro Frontend'. DONE-DEFINITION: (1) init() is replaced with registerRemotes() guarded by a module-level Set in MicroFrontendApp.tsx; (2) /store mounts the store remote and renders the product list; (3) /store/product/:id renders ProductDetail with product data; (4) a regression test covers the product detail route and a RUNTIME-010 e2e guard is added. TypeScript passes throughout.

userStories.title: Replace init() with registerRemotes() in MicroFrontendApp
userStories.description: As a developer, I want MicroFrontendApp.tsx to use registerRemotes() instead of init() so that the store and all other micro-frontends load without throwing #RUNTIME-010.
acceptanceCriteria: In `micro-frontends/host/src/microFe/MicroFrontendApp.tsx`: change `import { init, loadRemote }` to `import { registerRemotes, loadRemote }` from `@module-federation/runtime`
Add a module-level `const registeredRemotes = new Set<string>()` above the component definitions

Replace the entire `init({ name: '@ecommerce-host', remotes: [...] })` block (lines 93-102) with: if (!registeredRemotes.has(microFrontendConfig.remoteName)) { registerRemotes([{ name: microFrontendConfig.remoteName, entry: remoteUrl + '/remoteEntry.js' }]); registeredRemotes.add(microFrontendConfig.remoteName); }
No #RUNTIME-010 error appears in the browser console when navigating to /store
Typecheck passes (`tsc --noEmit -p micro-frontends/host/tsconfig.app.json`)
userStories.notes: Root cause confirmed by static analysis: MicroFrontendApp.tsx:17 imports `init`, line 93 calls it with name '@ecommerce-host'. The build-time MF runtime already owns name 'host' (module-federation.config.ts). Fix pattern documented in host/CLAUDE.md. This story applies the edit directly — no diagnosis step needed.

userStories.title: Verify store MFE mounts and product list renders at /store
userStories.description: As a shopper, I want navigating to /store to show the product list so I can browse products.
acceptanceCriteria: Serve the suite (`cd micro-frontends && npm run setup && npm start`) and open http://localhost:4200/store
The Spin loader resolves — no ErrorBoundaryFallback ('Failed to Load Micro Frontend') is shown — and the store remote mounts in its injected container
Browser console shows '[MicroFrontendApp] Successfully loaded store' with no #RUNTIME-010 error
The product grid (store/src/pages/ProductList.tsx) renders with products or correct loading/empty/error states
No regressions to the other remotes (checkout/account/admin) loading via the same MicroFrontendApp path
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser (screenshot of /store mounted for the progress log)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Dev serves on ports 4200-4204 (`npm start` in micro-frontends/). If gateway unavailable, set NX_USE_MOCK_DATA=true to test MFE load independently of backend. Keep registry/exposed-module names case-sensitive ('store' / 'ConsoleMicroApp').

userStories.title: Verify product detail page renders at /store/product/:id
userStories.description: As a shopper, I want clicking a product to navigate to its detail page so I can see product information and add it to my cart.
acceptanceCriteria: From /store, clicking a product card changes the host URL to /store/product/:id and renders ProductDetail (store/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx)
ProductDetail shows product name, price, description, and image gallery once useGetProductById resolves
A direct browser refresh of /store/product/:id also renders ProductDetail — TanStack Router basepath '/store' handles the sub-route correctly
With the Catalog service unavailable, the Empty fallback renders with a 'Back to Store' button (no blank page or crash)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser (screenshots of a product detail page for the progress log)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002. Route at store/src/routes/product/$id.tsx; TanStack Router uses basepath '/store' so /store/product/:id maps to internal /product/$id. Catalog API: GET /Catalog/GetProductById/:id via NX_API_BASE_URL (default http://localhost:8010). Use NX_USE_MOCK_DATA=true if backend unavailable.

userStories.title: Add product-detail route unit test and RUNTIME-010 e2e regression guard
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the product-detail route covered by a unit test and the RUNTIME-010 fix guarded by an e2e test so neither can silently break again.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `micro-frontends/store/src/routes/product/$id.test.tsx` modelled on `routes/index.test.tsx`: assert Route is defined, Route.options.component is defined, and Route.id equals '/product/$id'

Add a RUNTIME-010 regression test to `micro-frontends/e2e/module-federation.spec.ts`: navigate to /store, assert console contains '[MicroFrontendApp] Successfully loaded store' and does NOT contain '#RUNTIME-010' or 'name option cannot be changed', assert text 'Failed to Load Micro Frontend' is absent
`nx run store:test` passes with the new unit test
Append final outcome and learnings to progress.txt
Typecheck passes (`tsc --noEmit -p micro-frontends/store/tsconfig.app.json`)
Verify in browser (final screenshot of product list + product detail for the progress log)
userStories.notes: Independent of US-003 (can run in parallel). Unit test gap: routes/index.test.tsx exists but routes/product/$id.tsx has NO test. E2e gap: module-federation.spec.ts exists but has no RUNTIME-010 guard (confirmed by grep). Use the same Jest + @testing-library pattern as index.test.tsx.
